mission.title: dapr: Call existing gRPC methods and bring your own gRPC protos - renewed gRPC experience.
mission.description: Call existing gRPC methods and bring your own gRPC protos - renewed gRPC experience. . Requested by 9+ users.

steps.title: Check current dapr deployment
steps.description:
Verify your dapr version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
helm list -n dapr 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working dapr installation.

steps.title: Review dapr configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant dapr configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get configmap -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dapr
```
Today, Dapr requires users to implement a special gRPC service to receive calls and exposes a specialized endpoint to invoke generic gRPC endpoints.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Call existing gRPC methods and bring your own gRPC protos -…
steps.description:
This PR enables users to work with their existing gRPC protos for service invocation, while still utilizing Dapr's service invocation features like mTLS, API auth, telemetry, access lists etc.

### Notes for the reviewer

#### High level changes made in this PR

1. Access lists functionality *moved* from `pkg/configuration` to `pkg/acl`. This is for the better and ACLs now live in a dedicated and reusable package.
2. Adding Stream trace middleware for gRPC under `grpc_diagnostics.go` to support

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n dapr -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dapr
kubectl get events -n dapr --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Call existing gRPC methods and bring your own gRPC protos -…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: PR #3277 implemented a renewed gRPC experience that enables users to work with their existing gRPC protos for service invocation, while still utilizing Dapr's service invocation features like mTLS, API auth, telemetry, and access lists. This allows calling existing gRPC services through Dapr without needing to implement a specialized Dapr gRPC service interface.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with dapr installed or the issue environment reproducible.
